question.text: On expiry of commodity index options, which of the following is correct?

answer.text: All ITM contracts are auto-exercised unless buyer gives contrary instruction; all OTM contracts expire worthless
All contracts must be manually exercised by the buyer
All ITM and OTM contracts are automatically settled
ITM contracts are automatically closed only for institutional clients

explanation.text: **The Logic:** On expiry, all in-the-money (ITM) contracts are automatically exercised unless the buyer gives a 'contrary instruction.' All out-of-the-money (OTM) contracts expire worthless. **The Trap:** Manual exercise is not required (auto-exercise is the default). OTM contracts are NOT settled — they expire worthless. Auto-exercise applies to all clients, not just institutional. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers auto-exercise provisions for equity options. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 'contrary instruction' mechanism allows option buyers to choose not to exercise slightly ITM options where the gain doesn't cover transaction costs — useful in practice.
